core_mechanism: a finite-state Markov chain whose stationary distribution admits a closed product form only when the transition kernel satisfies detailed balance (is reversible), with the placement of a biasing factor inside the transition rates deciding whether reversibility—and hence the product-form law—survives
brief_reason: The abstract compares three update kernels that differ only in the stage at which a fitness bias enters the transition rates and shows that only the reversible kernel (Scheme III) yields a closed detailed-balance product-form stationary distribution while the others become nonreversible with no product form for m>=3.
